name: Tuna Thoran

name: Tsampa
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "300g Roasted barley flour (tsampa)", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "200ml Butter tea (po cha) or regular butter", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "40g Yak butter or regular butter", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "3g Salt", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "30g Dried cheese (chhurpi), crumbled (optional)", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "40g Dried apricots, chopped (optional)", "source_position": 6}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "To make butter tea (po cha) for mixing: brew strong black tea, add salt and yak butter (or regular butter), and churn vigorously until emulsified. Alternatively, use warm salted butter milk."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Place the roasted barley flour (tsampa) in a wide, deep bowl. Make a well in the centre."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Pour warm butter tea or warm salted butter into the well of the flour. Start with a small amount — about 100 ml."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Using your fingers, begin mixing the flour and liquid together in a circular motion, gradually incorporating more flour from the edges."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Continue adding butter tea gradually, kneading and mixing with your fingers until the mixture comes together into a stiff, dough-like consistency that can be shaped."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Knead the mixture in the bowl using your fingers and palm, pressing and folding, until it forms a smooth, cohesive ball. The traditional technique involves rolling the mixture against the side of the bowl."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Add crumbled dried cheese (chhurpi) and chopped dried apricots if using. Knead them into the tsampa ball."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Shape into small balls or a single large ball. Tsampa is eaten directly from the hand — pinch off pieces and eat."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Alternatively, tsampa can be mixed into a porridge consistency by adding more butter tea until it reaches a thick, smooth paste. Serve in bowls."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Serve immediately as a portable, energy-dense meal or snack."}]

name: Yakhni
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "750g Mutton (bone-in pieces)", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "400g Plain yogurt, whisked", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "15g Fennel powder", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "5g Dry ginger powder (saunth)", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "6piece Green cardamom", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "2piece Black cardamom", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "5piece Cloves", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "5g Cinnamon stick", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "2piece Bay leaf", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "5g Cumin seeds", "source_position": 10}, {"raw_text": "40ml Mustard oil", "source_position": 11}, {"raw_text": "12g Salt", "source_position": 12}, {"raw_text": "750ml Water", "source_position": 13}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Wash the mutton pieces thoroughly and drain well."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Heat mustard oil in a heavy-bottomed pot until it reaches smoking point, then reduce the heat."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Add cumin seeds, bay leaves, green cardamom, black cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon. Sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Add the mutton pieces and sear for 5–7 minutes, turning frequently."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Add water and salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Cover and cook for 45–60 minutes until the mutton is nearly tender."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "In a bowl, whisk the yogurt until completely smooth."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Add the fennel powder and dry ginger powder to the yogurt and mix well."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Reduce the heat to low. Gradually add the yogurt mixture to the pot, stirring continuously to prevent curdling."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Simmer gently for 15–20 minutes without allowing the curry to boil vigorously."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Continue cooking until the mutton is fully tender and the gravy becomes smooth and aromatic."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Taste and adjust salt if necessary."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Rest for 10 minutes before serving."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Serve hot with steamed rice."}]

name: Tandoori Roti
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "400g Whole wheat flour", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "220ml Water, warm", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "40g Butter or ghee", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "5g Salt", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "10ml Oil", "source_position": 5}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Combine whole wheat flour and salt. Gradually add warm water, kneading until a smooth, elastic dough forms, about 8 minutes."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Add oil and knead for another 2 minutes. Cover with damp cloth and rest 20 minutes."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Divide dough into 8 balls. Roll each into a thick disc, about 15 cm in diameter."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "If using a tandoor: stick the roti onto the inside wall of a preheated tandoor. Cook for 2–3 minutes until brown spots appear and the roti puffs slightly."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "If using a stovetop: heat a cast-iron tawa until very hot. Place roti on it. When bubbles form, flip and cook. Then place the tawa-cooked roti directly over the gas flame using tongs and cook until dark spots appear."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Apply butter or ghee generously while hot."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Serve immediately."}]

name: Shakarkandi Chaat
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "600g Sweet potatoes", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "30ml Lemon juice", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "10g Chaat masala", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "5g Roasted cumin powder", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "5g Red chilli powder", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "5g Black salt", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "20g Fresh coriander leaves", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "60ml Green chutney", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "40ml Tamarind chutney", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "50g Pomegranate seeds", "source_position": 10}]

Add lemon juice, black salt, chaat masala, cumin powder, and red chilli powder."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Toss gently to coat evenly."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Transfer to a serving plate."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Drizzle green chutney and tamarind chutney over the sweet potato cubes."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Sprinkle pomegranate seeds and chopped coriander on top."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Serve immediately while still warm."}]

name: Shahi Tukda
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "8piece White bread slices", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "1000ml Full-fat milk", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "150g Sugar", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "100ml Ghee for frying", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "0.5g Saffron strands", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "5g Cardamom powder", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "10ml Kewra water", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "10ml Rose water", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "30g Pistachios, slivered", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "30g Almonds, slivered", "source_position": 10}, {"raw_text": "100ml Sugar syrup", "source_position": 11}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Make rabri: bring milk to boil in a wide, heavy pan. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring frequently, until milk reduces to one-third its volume and thickens. Add sugar, saffron, cardamom, kewra, and rose water. Stir well. Cool slightly."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Remove crusts from bread slices. Cut each slice diagonally into two triangles."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Heat ghee in a shallow pan. name: Seekh Kebab

cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Combine all ingredients in a large bowl. name: Ram Ladoo

name: Phirni
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "1000ml Full-fat milk", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "60g Basmati rice, soaked and coarsely ground", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "120g Sugar", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "5g Cardamom powder", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "0.5g Saffron strands", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "15ml Rose water", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "25g Pistachios, slivered", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "25g Almonds, slivered", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "2piece Silver varq (edible silver leaf)", "source_position": 9}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. Drain and coarsely grind — not to a fine powder, but a grainy texture."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Bring milk to a boil in a heavy-bottomed pan."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Dissolve the coarsely ground rice in a small amount of cold milk to make a lump-free slurry."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Add the rice slurry to the boiling milk, stirring constantly to prevent lumps."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Reduce heat to low and cook, stirring continuously, for 15–20 minutes until the mixture thickens to a creamy porridge consistency."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Add sugar and stir until dissolved. name: Paranthe Wali Gali Paratha
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "400g Whole wheat flour", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "300g Potatoes, boiled and mashed", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "150g Paneer, grated", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "3piece Green chillies, finely chopped", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "20g Fresh coriander, chopped", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "5g Carom seeds (ajwain)", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "8g Red chilli powder", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "5g Garam masala", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "5g Dried mango powder (amchur)", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "80ml Ghee", "source_position": 10}, {"raw_text": "10g Salt", "source_position": 11}, {"raw_text": "200ml Water", "source_position": 12}]

name: Paranthas
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "300g Whole wheat flour (atta)", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "300g Potatoes, boiled and mashed", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "80g Onion, finely chopped", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "2piece Green chillies, finely chopped", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "20g Fresh coriander leaves, chopped", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "10g Ginger, grated", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "4g Cumin seeds", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "3g Carom seeds (ajwain)", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "4g Red chilli powder", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "3g Garam masala", "source_position": 10}, {"raw_text": "3g Amchur (dry mango powder)", "source_position": 11}, {"raw_text": "8g Salt", "source_position": 12}, {"raw_text": "80g Ghee or butter", "source_position": 13}, {"raw_text": "150ml Water", "source_position": 14}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Make the dough: combine whole wheat flour, carom seeds, a pinch of salt, and 1 teaspoon of ghee in a bowl. Gradually add water and knead for 5–6 minutes into a smooth, soft, pliable dough. Cover with a damp cloth and rest for 20 minutes."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Prepare the aloo filling: in a bowl, combine the mashed potatoes, finely chopped onion, green chillies, grated ginger, coriander leaves, cumin seeds, red chilli powder, garam masala, amchur, and salt. name: Nahari
